goal: Land the `quality_pipeline` sub-module of `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` that pulls LSP diagnostics through the existing `DiagnosticsClient` (WO-0015) and persists them as rows in the ¬ß12.1 `quality_issues` SQLite table via `KnowledgeGraph::execute_in_transaction`, completing the diag-bridge ‚Üí G7 quality feed described in master-plan ¬ß13.5 line 1437.
plan_summary: Build on WO-0015's `DiagnosticsClient` + `SerenaClient` trait and WO-0011's `KnowledgeGraph` (¬ß12.1 schema including `quality_issues`) to wire the G7 pipeline. New module `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs` exposes (1) `persist_diagnostics(client: &DiagnosticsClient, kg: &mut KnowledgeGraph, file_uri: Url, language: Language) -> Result<usize, QualityPipelineError>` ‚Äî async, fetches diagnostics via `DiagnosticsClient::diagnostics`, converts each `lsp_types::Diagnostic` into a `quality_issues` INSERT row (file_path, line_start, line_end, category, severity, message, rule_id, source_tool, first_seen=now), wraps ALL inserts in ONE `kg.execute_in_transaction(‚Ä¶)` call so the write is atomic and BEGIN IMMEDIATE is honoured; (2) pure helpers `severity_to_quality(lsp_types::DiagnosticSeverity) -> &'static str` and `category_from_severity(lsp_types::DiagnosticSeverity) -> &'static str` with documented mappings (Error‚Üí"high"/"type_error", Warning‚Üí"medium"/"lint", Information‚Üí"low"/"lint", Hint‚Üí"info"/"lint") ‚Äî picks the five-level ¬ß12.1 enum over LSP's four-level spec, rationale in rustdoc; (3) `source_tool` string built as `format!("lsp:{}", diag.source.as_deref().unwrap_or_else(|| language_default_server(language)))` where `language_default_server` returns `"pyright"`/`"rust-analyzer"`/`"tsserver"`/etc. for the Language enum. Module-root tests drive a `FakeSerenaClient` (the same DEC-0008 dependency-inversion seam WO-0015 introduced) that returns canned `lsp_types::Diagnostic` payloads, and assert the rows land in `quality_issues` with the correct projection of every column. `KnowledgeGraph` is opened on a `tempfile::TempDir`-backed path so the test exercises the real ¬ß12.1 DDL and real WAL pragmas ‚Äî no in-memory shortcut, no `rusqlite` mock. The function does NOT upsert on re-ingest at this WO time ‚Äî identical diagnostics seen twice produce two rows; dedup is P1-W5-F08 territory and listed as scope_out.
scope_in: crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/lib.rs ‚Äî add `pub mod quality_pipeline;` + re-export `persist_diagnostics`, `QualityPipelineError` (and any helper types the executor deems public)

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml ‚Äî add `ucil-core = { path = "../ucil-core" }` is already present; add `rusqlite = { workspace = true }` ONLY if the module needs to name `rusqlite::Error` types in its error enum (if it goes solely through `KnowledgeGraphError`, the dep is not required); the executor must prefer `ucil-core`'s types to avoid a duplicate edge

crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml ‚Äî `[dev-dependencies]`: add `tempfile = { workspace = true }` (already workspace-dep'd, used by `ucil-core` tests) so the module-root test can open a real on-disk `KnowledgeGraph`
Module-root tests in `quality_pipeline.rs` (flat ‚Äî NOT under `mod tests { }` ‚Äî per DEC-0005):

* `test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues` ‚Äî FakeSerenaClient returns 3 canned LSP Diagnostic values (one Error, one Warning, one Hint across two files), open `KnowledgeGraph` on a tempdir, call `persist_diagnostics` twice (for two different file URIs), assert 3 rows total in `quality_issues`, assert every row's file_path+line_start+line_end+category+severity+message+rule_id+source_tool column matches the canned diagnostic's projection. This test's leaf name is the one the P1-W5-F05 acceptance selector matches (`-p ucil-lsp-diagnostics test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues`).
* `test_persist_empty_diagnostics_returns_zero` ‚Äî FakeSerenaClient returns `vec![]`; `persist_diagnostics` returns `Ok(0)` and `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM quality_issues` is 0.
* `test_severity_mapping_covers_all_lsp_levels` ‚Äî unit-test for `severity_to_quality` over all four `lsp_types::DiagnosticSeverity` variants, assert each maps to the documented string.
* `test_category_mapping_covers_all_lsp_levels` ‚Äî unit-test for `category_from_severity` over all four variants, assert each maps to the documented string.

* `test_source_tool_falls_back_to_language_default` ‚Äî diagnostic with `source=None` produces `source_tool="lsp:pyright"` (for Language::Python) / `"lsp:rust-analyzer"` (for Language::Rust); diagnostic with `source=Some("ruff")` produces `source_tool="lsp:ruff"`.

The test-side `FakeSerenaClient` lives in a `#[cfg(test)]` sub-mod inside `quality_pipeline.rs` (mirroring WO-0015's approach in `diagnostics.rs`). It implements UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait and records nothing the test does not assert. No wire-protocol mock of Serena MCP is introduced.

All `.await` in `persist_diagnostics` that hit the `SerenaClient` go through `DiagnosticsClient::diagnostics` which already wraps in `tokio::time::timeout(LSP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS)` ‚Äî this WO adds NO second timeout layer (re-wrapping would be harmful).

Rustdoc on every public item: `persist_diagnostics`, `QualityPipelineError`, `severity_to_quality`, `category_from_severity`, `language_default_server` (if made pub), with a module-level rustdoc explaining the LSP-4‚Üíquality-5 collapse and citing ¬ß12.1.

`#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]` clean on all new code; use the same `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` escape hatch as `diagnostics.rs` if a `QualityPipelineError` name inside `quality_pipeline` trips pedantic.

`tracing` spans: the async body of `persist_diagnostics` opens one span named `ucil.lsp.persist_diagnostics` per master-plan ¬ß15.2 naming convention (`ucil.<layer>.<op>`); each INSERT loop iteration is a `tracing::debug!` event, not a new span.
scope_out: Populating call-hierarchy / type-hierarchy edges ‚Äî P1-W5-F06 territory
Standalone LSP subprocess spawning ‚Äî P1-W5-F07 territory
Docker-backed end-to-end Serena fixture test that validates 'within 5s of daemon start' wall-time ‚Äî P1-W5-F08 territory; this WO's acceptance selector is unit-scoped

Upsert / dedup on identical diagnostics (first_seen vs last_seen semantics) ‚Äî gated to a future WO; this WO inserts fresh rows every call and documents the choice in the `persist_diagnostics` rustdoc
`resolved` column update flow (quality_issues.resolved = 1 when the diagnostic disappears) ‚Äî future WO
Wiring the pipeline into the daemon's file-watcher callback chain ‚Äî out of scope for P1-W5-F05; a future daemon-integration WO will call `persist_diagnostics` from the watcher on PostToolUse
Any modification to `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/diagnostics.rs` (WO-0015's surface) ‚Äî this WO only consumes it
Any modification to `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs` (WO-0014's surface) ‚Äî this WO does not touch the bridge

Any modification to `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/types.rs` ‚Äî this WO uses `lsp_types` on the wire, not the bridge-local `Diagnostic` placeholder; the docstring on `types.rs::Diagnostic` explicitly flags the adapter as F05's concern and this WO honours that by NOT introducing the adapter here (the `quality_issues` row is built directly from `lsp_types::Diagnostic`)
Any schema change to `quality_issues` ‚Äî ¬ß12.1 is frozen at WO-0011
Adding `ucil-daemon` as a dep of `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` ‚Äî DEC-0008 forbids the cycle

New ADRs ‚Äî the severity/category mapping is small enough to justify in rustdoc; if the executor discovers a genuine ambiguity (e.g. `rusqlite` version churn, `lsp-types` severity field gone optional in a new release), they must pause and write a proposed ADR rather than silently picking
acceptance: New `quality_pipeline` module exists at `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/quality_pipeline.rs` and is declared + re-exported from `lib.rs`
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues` matches ‚â• 1 test, all passing (the P1-W5-F05 acceptance selector)
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics quality_pipeline::` matches ‚â• 5 tests, all passing
`cargo clippy -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean (pedantic + nursery)
`cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps` is clean (no broken intra-doc links)
Reality-check oracle: stashing `quality_pipeline.rs` causes the `test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues` test to fail (selector misses on stashed state), popping restores it
No mocks of `rusqlite::Connection`, Serena MCP wire protocol, or LSP subprocesses in the diff ‚Äî the `FakeSerenaClient` pattern is the DEC-0008 seam and is explicitly allowed
`persist_diagnostics` writes through `KnowledgeGraph::execute_in_transaction` (one call per invocation, all diagnostics inserted atomically)
`LspDiagnosticsBridge` and `DiagnosticsClient` public surfaces from WO-0014/WO-0015 remain byte-for-byte unchanged (git diff proves it)
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -E '^\s+PASS' | awk 'END{exit (NR < 1)}'
cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics 'quality_pipeline::' --status-level=pass --hide-progress-bar 2>&1 | grep -E '^\s+PASS' | awk 'END{exit (NR < 5)}'
cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps 2>&1 | grep -qE '^(warning|error)' && exit 1 || cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps
context_refs: master-plan:¬ß12.1 Knowledge graph schema ‚Äî quality_issues table (lines 1196-1212)
master-plan:¬ß13.5 How it feeds other groups ‚Äî G7 quality_issues (line 1437)
master-plan:¬ß18 Phase 1 Week 5 ‚Äî line 1761 (wire diagnostics bridge ‚Üí G7 quality)
lessons_applied: Module-root test placement so the frozen nextest selector `test_diagnostics_to_quality_issues` resolves without relying on substring-match into a `mod tests { ‚Ä¶ }` wrapper (WO-0006/WO-0007 selector-mismatch lesson; DEC-0005)

No mocking of Serena MCP or rusqlite ‚Äî the `FakeSerenaClient` implements UCIL's own `SerenaClient` trait (DEC-0008 seam) and the test opens a real `KnowledgeGraph` on a `tempfile::TempDir` so ¬ß12.1 DDL + WAL + busy_timeout pragmas all run (phase-log invariant 1)
All writes go through `KnowledgeGraph::execute_in_transaction` so ¬ß11 BEGIN IMMEDIATE discipline is preserved (rust-style.md; knowledge_graph.rs:417-440 contract)

Do NOT wrap the `DiagnosticsClient::diagnostics` call in a second `tokio::time::timeout` ‚Äî the client already wraps at 5 s (`LSP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS`); a second wrapper would mask the typed `Timeout` error (WO-0015 surface contract)
`thiserror` + `#[non_exhaustive]` on `QualityPipelineError`; never `anyhow` in library code (rust-style.md Errors ¬ß)
Do NOT add `ucil-daemon` as a dependency of `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` (DEC-0008 ¬ßConsequences ‚Äî cycle forbidden)
Do NOT modify `diagnostics.rs`, `bridge.rs`, or `types.rs` ‚Äî those are frozen by prior WOs and the `git diff == 0` acceptance checks enforce it

If the severity/category mapping turns out to be contested (e.g. user pushes back on Error‚Üíhigh vs Error‚Üícritical), pause and write a proposed ADR rather than silently picking ‚Äî this WO documents the default choice in rustdoc but does not promote it to an ADR at this time
